title: Speaking - Part 2
description: Compare the two photographs and answer the question. You have about one minute.

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what causes and effects of rural poverty these images suggest and what challenges people living in rural poverty face.

images.label: A
images.description: A family in a remote rural village living in a simple thatched dwelling, with no electricity visible, children playing outside in dusty conditions

images.label: B
images.description: A rural school with overcrowded classrooms, children sharing textbooks and sitting on the floor, a teacher writing on a cracked blackboard

followUp.question: What do you think are the most significant barriers preventing people in rural poverty from improving their situation?

tasks.instructions: Compare the two photographs and say what factors contribute to urban homelessness and what society could do to address the problem.

images.label: D
images.description: A homeless person sleeping on a city pavement in a sleeping bag, passers-by walking past without making eye contact

images.label: E
images.description: Volunteers distributing hot food and blankets to homeless people outside a soup kitchen on a winter evening, people queuing gratefully

followUp.question: Do you think homelessness is primarily a result of personal circumstances or systemic social failures? Why?
